questions.question: Find \(\frac{dy}{dx}\) if \(y = (3x^2 + 1)\sin(2x)\)
questions.explanation: Using the product rule: u = 3x²+1, u' = 6x; v = sin(2x), v' = 2cos(2x). dy/dx = u'v + uv' = 6x·sin(2x) + (3x²+1)·2cos(2x) = 6x·sin(2x) + 2(3x²+1)cos(2x)

questions.question: Find \(\frac{dy}{dx}\) if \(y = e^{x^2}\ln(1 + x)\)
questions.explanation: Using the product rule: u = e^(x²), u' = 2x·e^(x²); v = ln(1+x), v' = 1/(1+x). dy/dx = 2x·e^(x²)·ln(1+x) + e^(x²)·1/(1+x) = e^(x²)[2x·ln(1+x) + 1/(1+x)]

questions.question: Find \(\frac{dy}{dx}\) if \(y = x^2 e^{\sin x}\)
questions.explanation: Using the product rule: u = x², u' = 2x; v = e^(sin x), v' = e^(sin x)·cos x. dy/dx = 2x·e^(sin x) + x²·e^(sin x)·cos x = e^(sin x)(2x + x²·cos x)

questions.question: Find \(\frac{dy}{dx}\) if \(y = (x^2 + 4x)\tan(3x)\)
questions.explanation: Using the product rule: u = x²+4x, u' = 2x+4; v = tan(3x), v' = 3sec²(3x). dy/dx = (2x+4)·tan(3x) + (x²+4x)·3sec²(3x) = (2x+4)tan(3x) + 3(x²+4x)sec²(3x)

questions.question: Find \(\frac{dy}{dx}\) if \(y = x\cos(x^2)\)
questions.explanation: Using the product rule: u = x, u' = 1; v = cos(x²), v' = -2x·sin(x²). dy/dx = 1·cos(x²) + x·[-2x·sin(x²)] = cos(x²) - 2x²·sin(x²)

questions.question: Find \(\frac{dy}{dx}\) if \(y = \sqrt{x^2 + 1}\sin(5x)\)
questions.explanation: Using the product rule: u = √(x²+1), u' = x/√(x²+1); v = sin(5x), v' = 5cos(5x). dy/dx = [x/√(x²+1)]·sin(5x) + √(x²+1)·5cos(5x) = [x·sin(5x)]/√(x²+1) + 5√(x²+1)cos(5x)

questions.question: Find \(\frac{dy}{dx}\) if \(y = (3x^2 - x)e^{\cos x}\)
questions.explanation: Using the product rule: u = 3x²-x, u' = 6x-1; v = e^(cos x), v' = -e^(cos x)·sin x. dy/dx = (6x-1)·e^(cos x) + (3x²-x)·[-e^(cos x)·sin x] = e^(cos x)[(6x-1) - (3x²-x)sin x]

questions.question: Find \(\frac{dy}{dx}\) if \(y = \ln(1 + x^2)\tan^{-1}(2x)\)
questions.explanation: Using the product rule: u = ln(1+x²), u' = 2x/(1+x²); v = tan⁻¹(2x), v' = 2/(1+4x²). dy/dx = [2x/(1+x²)]·tan⁻¹(2x) + ln(1+x²)·[2/(1+4x²)]

questions.question: Find \(\frac{dy}{dx}\) if \(y = (x^2 + 3x)e^{x^3}\)
questions.explanation: Using the product rule: u = x²+3x, u' = 2x+3; v = e^(x³), v' = 3x²·e^(x³). dy/dx = (2x+3)·e^(x³) + (x²+3x)·3x²·e^(x³) = e^(x³)[2x+3+3x⁴+9x³] = e^(x³)(3x⁴+9x³+2x+3)
